ddls:
CREATE TABLE "emails" (
  "email_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject line of the email */,
  "from_address" TEXT /* Email address of the sender */,
  "to_address" TEXT /* Email address(es) of the primary recipient(s) */,
  "cc_address" TEXT /* Email address(es) of the CC recipient(s) */,
  "bcc_address" TEXT /* Email address(es) of the BCC recipient(s) */,
  "folder" TEXT /* Folder where the email is stored */,
  "origin" TEXT /* Origin of the email (system or user-generated) */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* File name of the email attachment, if any */,
  "content" TEXT /* Body content of the email */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the sender */,
  "labeled" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the email is labeled for importance */,
  "dollar_value" REAL /* Dollar value mentioned in the email, if any */,
  "message_length" INTEGER /* Length of the email message in characters */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the email was sent */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the email thread */,
  "email_type" TEXT /* Type of email (e.g., meeting invite, reply, forward) */,
  "sensitivity_level" TEXT /* Sensitivity level of the email */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the email has been read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("email_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_folder FOREIGN KEY ("folder") REFERENCES folders ("folder_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department where the user works */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "folders" (
  "folder_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each folder */,
  "folder_name" TEXT /* Name of the folder */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the folder's purpose */,
  "parent_folder_id" INTEGER /* Parent folder ID */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the folder creator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("folder_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_folders_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_folders_parent_folder_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_folder_id") REFERENCES folders ("folder_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email to which the attachment belongs */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the attachment file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the attachment in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the attachment was uploaded */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of attachment file */,
  "download_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the attachment has been downloaded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "labels" (
  "label_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each label */,
  "label_name" TEXT /* Name of the label */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the label */,
  "label_color" TEXT /* Color assigned to the label */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_categories" (
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email being categorized */,
  "label_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding label */,
  "category_weight" REAL /* Weight or importance of the label */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("email_id", "label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dollar_mentions" (
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dollar mention record */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email containing the dollar mention */,
  "dollar_value" REAL /* The dollar amount mentioned in the email */,
  "context" TEXT /* Context or description of how the dollar amount is referenced */,
  "context_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the context */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mention_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dollar_mentions_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "email_id" INTEGER /* ID of the email corresponding to the log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user taking the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken on the email (e.g., sent, received, deleted) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "log_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the action logged */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_logs_email_id FOREIGN KEY ("email_id") REFERENCES emails ("email_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager overseeing the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Full name of the manager */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the manager */,
  "manager_title" TEXT /* Title of the manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic record */,
  "total_emails_sent" INTEGER /* Total number of emails sent */,
  "total_emails_received" INTEGER /* Total number of emails received */,
  "average_response_time" REAL /* Average response time to emails in hours */,
  "time_period" TEXT /* Time period for the statistics (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_templates" (
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email template */,
  "template_name" TEXT /* Name of the template */,
  "template_content" TEXT /* Content of the template for reusability */,
  "template_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the template's purpose */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("template_id")
)
